name: Jollibee Times Square
description: Iconic Filipino fast-food chain loved by Filipinos worldwide. Famous for Chickenjoy and palabok.

name: Ihawan Restaurant
description: Beloved Filipino BBQ spot in Woodside serving classic grilled meats, adobo, and sinigang.

name: Renee's Kitchenette & Grill
description: Homestyle Filipino cooking in the heart of Woodside. Try the kare-kare and lechon kawali.

name: Phil-Am Food Mart
description: Premier Filipino grocery store stocking Philippine imports, fresh produce, and specialty ingredients.

name: Seafood City Supermarket
description: Large Filipino supermarket chain with a wide selection of Filipino products, fresh seafood, and a food court.

name: FilAm Arts - Filipino American Arts Exposition
description: Cultural organization celebrating Filipino arts, heritage events, and community gatherings in NYC.

name: Woodside - Little Manila
description: The heart of Filipino New York. Roosevelt Avenue from 69th to 72nd Street is lined with Filipino restaurants, bakeries, remittance centers, and grocery stores.
subtitle: Little Manila - Queens
filipinoPresence: The largest concentration of Filipino-owned businesses in NYC and a major center for Filipino Independence Day celebrations and community parades.

name: Krystal's Cafe
description: Cozy Filipino cafe known for halo-halo, pan de sal, and traditional pastries.

name: Anak Filipino Kitchen
description: Modern Filipino restaurant in Brooklyn serving contemporary takes on classic Filipino dishes.

name: Philippine Consulate General NYC
description: Official Philippine government consulate serving Filipinos in New York with passport, visa, and community services.

name: Engeline's Restaurant
description: Family-run Filipino restaurant serving authentic home-style cooking. Known for their bangus and dinuguan.

name: Ridgewood Filipino Community Center
description: Community hub offering language classes, cultural events, and support services for Filipino-Americans.

name: Jackson Heights, Queens
description: Adjacent to Woodside, Jackson Heights is one of the most ethnically diverse neighborhoods in the world, with a significant Filipino population alongside South Asian and Latin American communities.
subtitle: Diverse & Vibrant - Queens
filipinoPresence: Several Filipino restaurants, markets, and services are scattered throughout, with quick access to Woodside's Little Manila strip via the 7 train.

name: Jersey City, NJ
description: A thriving Filipino community across the Hudson, where many Filipinos live while working in Manhattan and stay connected through restaurants, churches, and cultural events.
subtitle: Greater Metro Area - New Jersey
filipinoPresence: Filipino restaurants, churches, and the annual Jersey City Filipino Festival draw thousands of attendees each year.

name: Manhattan (Midtown)
description: A work and commerce hub for Filipinos visiting, commuting through, or working in the city, anchored by Jollibee Times Square and the Philippine Consulate General.
subtitle: Work & Commerce - Manhattan
filipinoPresence: The Philippine Consulate General at 556 5th Ave serves thousands of Filipinos monthly with passport, visa, and overseas worker services.

name: Brooklyn
description: A growing Filipino presence, especially around areas like Bay Ridge and Sunset Park, with modern restaurants and second-generation Filipino-American culture.
subtitle: Growing Community - Brooklyn
filipinoPresence: Younger Filipino-Americans and new Filipino restaurants have energized Brooklyn's Filipino dining scene with contemporary takes on classic flavors.

name: The Bronx
description: A borough with dedicated Filipino community roots, where churches, community centers, and local gatherings help families stay connected.
subtitle: Community Roots - The Bronx
filipinoPresence: Filipino Catholic churches and community organizations provide social services, cultural programming, and a sense of home for Fil-Am families.
